title: career-ops.steering.answered v1
description: Emitted by career-ops when the user answers a behavioural-steering clarifying question (e.g. target geography, salary floor, role-fit tradeoffs) during a session. These answers feed the auto-memory layer at ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ that is reserved for behavioural steering only. Consumers use this to keep steering state in sync without re-reading transcripts. SENSITIVE-DATA NOTE: this event lands in the shared nervous-bus debug log (~/.cache/nervous-bus/debug.jsonl — world-tailed into Redis for any consumer). Publishers MUST NOT put verbatim personal detail (exact salary figures, addresses, full risk-tolerance narrative) in `answer`; prefer a short category-level label plus a `memory_file` pointer to the durable, access-controlled record. `answer` is intentionally optional and length-capped to discourage using it as a verbatim-content sink.

question.description: The clarifying/steering question posed to the user.

answer.description: Optional short paraphrase/label of the answer (NOT verbatim sensitive detail — see schema-level SENSITIVE-DATA NOTE). Use `memory_file` to point at the full, access-controlled record instead of inlining it here.

category.description: Which steering dimension this answer updates.

session_id.description: Identifier of the career-ops conversation/session this exchange occurred in.

memory_file.description: Auto-memory file the answer was written into, if any (e.g. user_job_search.md). Preferred over `answer` for anything sensitive.

supersedes.description: true if this answer overrides a previously recorded steering value for the same category.
